The '''Battle of Bal'demnic''' was a conflict that occurred during the third and final year of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], instigated by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]' attempt to mine [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] from [[Bal'demnic/Legends|Bal'demnic's]] crust. A [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] task force headed by [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Halagad Ventor]] and [[Sha Koon]] was dispatched to prevent the mining.

==Background==
[[File:DarthTandP.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Sith Lords Darth Tenebrous and Plagueis (both pictured) were informed of Bal'demnic's cortosis deposits in the decades before the Clone Wars.]]
In [[67 BBY/Legends|67 BBY]], [[Subtext Mining]] provided [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lords of the Sith]] [[Darth Tenebrous/Legends|Darth Tenebrous]] and [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Darth Plagueis]] with secret intel revealing large deposits of cortosis ore within the planet's crust. While investigating this claim, Plagueis murdered his master on Bal'demnic, and glimpsed this battle in a vision of the future. Upon his return to [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]], Plagueis entrusted [[Larsh Hill]] to purchase large amounts of Bal'demnic land and acquire mining rights for the cortosis-rich mainland.<ref name="Darth Plagueis">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]</ref> Many years later during the [[Outer Rim Sieges/Legends|Outer Rim Sieges]], the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy]] moved in with their mining operations and military.<ref name="DB Bal'demnic" /> This maneuver also facilitated the Confederacy's strategy to lure Republic forces away from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] during the final stages of the war.<ref name="TEA" />

In the face of the Confederate armed forces, the native [[Kon'me]] leaders were given little choice but to allow a major mining operation to proceed.<ref name="DB Bal'demnic" /> Jedi intelligence managed to intercept leaked Confederacy communications, which alerted them to the situation on Bal'demnic. [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] knew full well that if cortosis fell into the hands of Confederacy, the ramifications for the Jedi would be disastrous. The [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] appointed [[Halagad Ventor]] and [[Sha Koon]] to lead a battalion of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] with several other Jedi in an important mission to sabotage the mining operation.<ref name="DB Bal'demnic" />

==The battle==
[[File:Konme-CWChapter22.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Kon'me defend their planet from the Separatists Droid Army.]]
Although the native Kon'me leaders allowed the Confederacy mining operations to begin, the citizens were not so easy to persuade. An uprising ensued, and the Kon'me began attacking the Confederate forces with powerful weapons provided by war profiteers.<ref name="DB Bal'demnic" /> The Confederacy responded, unleashing their [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]]s upon the rebellious Kon'me. [[OG-9 homing spider droid/Legends|OG-9 homing spider droids]] scaled the planet's cliffs and destroyed many of the natives' weapon emplacements.<ref name="CW22" /> Despite their heavy losses, the Kon'me were able to hold their own against the mechanical intruders, and a full-fledged battle erupted.<ref name="DB Bal'demnic" />

When Halagad Ventor's Republic task force arrived in the skies of Bal'demnic, his [[Aggressive ReConnaissance-170 starfighter/Legends|ARC-170 starfighters]] met immediate resistance in the form of Confederacy [[Droid tri-fighter/Legends|Droid tri-fighters]].<ref name="CW22" /> When the air skirmish was over, Halagad and his troops landed with the intent to assist the natives.<ref name="DB Bal'demnic" /> With all these foreign forces on their planet, however, the natives were further enraged to the point of total frenzy. In their final bid to drive the outlanders off their precious world, the Kon'me did not differentiate between Republic and Confederate forces in their powerful counter-attack. With the Confederate forces now under fire from two sides, and also being hindered by the planet's difficult terrain, they made a full retreat. Ventor and Koon, who had no desire to return fire on the hostile natives, also ordered a retreat of their forces.<ref name="DB Bal'demnic" />

==Aftermath==
The Republic left a small fleet in Bal'demnic's orbit, establishing a planetary defense blockade to prevent any further Confederacy intrusions.<ref name="DB Bal'demnic" />

==Behind the scenes==
The battle was first briefly glimpsed in [[Chapter 22 (Star Wars: Clone Wars)|''Clone Wars Chapter 22'']]. [[Arthur Papadam]] established the details of the battle in [[Bal'demnic/Legends|Bal'demnic's]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry, via the ''[[What's The Story?]]'' campaign. [[James Luceno]] further elaborated upon the battle's background in [[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']].
